title: Fix Staff Management Page crash on 'Add Staff'

requirements.text: Fix the crash/error in `frontend/src/pages/hr/AddEmployeePage.tsx` that occurs when the HR Admin clicks the 'Add Staff' button on the staff management page. Investigate and resolve all runtime errors including: null/undefined actor access before initialization, type mismatches on the `directManagerId` field or manager dropdown data, missing or broken form field bindings, unhandled promise rejections from the `createEmployeeWithAccount` (or equivalent) mutation, and any broken imports or missing component references. The form must open and render without errors, all fields (including the Direct Manager dropdown and Username/Password fields) must populate correctly, and submission must complete without throwing an unhandled exception.

quotes: Fix the staff management page when hitting add staff

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Add Staff' on the HR Employees page opens the AddEmployeePage form without any crash or error screen.
The Direct Manager dropdown populates with active manager-role employees without throwing a runtime error.
All form fields (name, email, department, role, username, password, directManagerId, etc.) are bound correctly and accept user input.
Submitting the form with valid data calls the backend employee creation endpoint without an unhandled exception.
Inline validation errors are shown for empty required fields without crashing.
A success toast is displayed and the user is navigated back to the employee list on successful submission.
No TypeScript compilation errors exist in AddEmployeePage.tsx.

requirements.text: In `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`, audit and fix the mutation hook used by `AddEmployeePage.tsx` (e.g., `useCreateEmployeeWithAccount` or equivalent). Ensure it guards against a null/undefined actor before calling the backend, correctly maps the backend `#ok`/`#err` response variants to resolved/rejected Promise states, and invalidates the employees query cache on success so the employee list refreshes automatically.

quotes: Fix the staff management page when hitting add staff

acceptanceCriteria: The mutation hook does not throw if the actor is null or undefined — it rejects with a descriptive error message instead.
A backend `#ok` response resolves the Promise and triggers employees query cache invalidation.
A backend `#err` response rejects the Promise with the error text, which is surfaced to the form as an inline error.
No unhandled promise rejections occur during the mutation call.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not alter the backend data model or endpoints unless strictly required to fix a type mismatch causing the frontend crash.
nonGoals: Adding new fields or features to the Add Staff form beyond what is already specified.
Changing the visual design or layout of the Add Staff page.
Fixing unrelated pages or features not involved in the Add Staff flow.
